mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future earnings and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 5-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: A sudden spike in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points within a single month signals abrupt labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE spikes > 0.5pp, or the signal loses predictive power during non-recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand recovery, which sustains corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.04, or the signal reverses during inflationary demand surges.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in industrial production during a late-cycle expansion indicate manufacturing contraction and supply chain bottlenecks, which compress profit margins and drive negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late-cycle phases produce positive 20-day forward returns in > 60% of cases, or the predictive relationship vanishes when CPIAUCSL growth remains below 2%.

mechanism_narrative: A widening gap between the federal funds rate and core inflation creates positive real yields that stabilize currency purchasing power, prompting institutional capital rotation into equities and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Positive real yield spreads fail to predict positive 5-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.10) over a 3-year walk-forward test, or the signal breaks when liquidity conditions tighten.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent month-over-month declines in total nonfarm payrolls reflect weakening labor demand and reduced household income, which systematically erodes corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS declines show no consistent negative correlation with 60-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.01) across multiple economic cycles, or the signal is overwhelmed by monetary policy interventions.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.5% confirms robust aggregate demand and economic expansion, which validates earnings forecasts and triggers sustained equity buying pressure, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: GDP growth above 2.5% fails to generate positive 20-day forward returns in > 50% of observations, or the predictive accuracy drops below chance during stagflationary episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between falling industrial output and rising unemployment during a recessionary phase signals severe economic contraction, forcing rapid deleveraging and defensive portfolio shifts that drive negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The INDPRO-UNRATE divergence fails to predict negative 5-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.03 during recessions, or equity markets rally on anticipated policy stimulus despite the divergence.
